achievements: Implemented core/security/jwt_handler.py with create_access_token, decode_access_token, generate_refresh_token
Added core/middleware/auth/dependencies.py with get_current_user, RoleChecker, invalidate_user_cache
Added refresh_tokens migration (core/database/migrations/SQL/add_refresh_tokens.sql) and registered in migration_history.py
Created core/database/dao/refresh_tokens.py DAO (create, find_active, revoke, revoke_all_for_user)
Hardened decode_access_token and added unit test for missing JWT_SECRET_KEY validation
Added tests/conftest.py and extended tests/integration/conftest.py for new auth fixtures
improvements: Wire refresh_tokens DAO into a /auth/refresh endpoint and rotate refresh tokens on use
Persist token hashes (sha256) in refresh_tokens rather than raw tokens to limit DB-leak blast radius
Add invalidate_user_cache() calls to every code path that mutates user role/status
Centralize JWT config (secret, algorithm, TTLs) in a single settings module instead of os.getenv scattered reads
Add structured custom_log calls on auth failures (bad token, expired, revoked) for firewall correlation
Type-annotate RoleChecker/MinRoleChecker return types and add docstrings on the public auth dependencies
next_tasks: Implement POST /api/v1/auth/refresh using refresh_tokens DAO with rotation + reuse detection
Implement POST /api/v1/auth/logout that calls revoke / revoke_all_for_user and invalidates Redis user cache
Add integration tests under tests/integration/ for login → refresh → logout → refresh-after-revoke flow
Add unit tests for RefreshToken DAO (create, find_active expiry, revoke idempotency, revoke_all_for_user)
Run python db_migrate.py against the test DB to confirm add_refresh_tokens.sql applies cleanly
Update .wolf/cerebrum.md with the auth conventions established this week (JWT secret required, cache-invalidate on user mutation)
risks: refresh_tokens table likely stores raw tokens; a DB read leak would grant session takeover — switch to hashed storage before shipping
No background sweeper for expired/revoked refresh_tokens — table will grow unbounded; needs a periodic cleanup job or partial index
60s Redis user cache means role demotions take up to a minute to take effect unless invalidate_user_cache is called everywhere — easy to miss
JWT_SECRET_KEY validation only triggers on decode path; ensure it also fails fast at startup in server.py to avoid first-request 500s
tests/unit/test_api_v1_auth_router.py was edited repeatedly (>2x) in one session — per OpenWolf rules this should be logged to .wolf/buglog.json
Integration tests require live Postgres+Redis; CI (.github/workflows/test.yml) must provision them or the new auth flows won't be covered
No rate limiting specifically on /auth/login or /auth/refresh beyond the generic firewall — credential stuffing risk
